statement.p: You will build two identical rectangular enclosures next to a each other, sharing a side. You have a total of 372 feet of fence to use. Find the dimensions of each pen such that you can enclose the maximum possible area. One approach is to let x represent the length of fencing that the two pens share, and write a formula for the total area of the enclosures. Then find its vertex and interpret it.
The length of each pen (along the wall that they share) should be , the width should be , and the maximum possible area of each pen is .

solution.p: Drawing a diagram should help us write an expression for the area of the pen.
Let the width be w ft, and the length be \ell ft. Since there is a total of 372 feet of fence, we have:
If each pens length is \ell feet, then its width is 93-0.75\ell feet. Now we can write an expression for the area of each pen:
Identify that a=-0.75, b=93, c=0. The maximum area happens at the parabolas vertex.
To find the vertex, we first find the parabolas axis of symmetry by using the axis formula:
Next, we substitute \ell= 62 into the expression, and we have:

The parabolas vertex is at {\left(62,2883\right)}. This implies that each pens maximum area is {2883} square feet. This happens when the length is {62\ {\rm ft}} feet, and the width is 93 - 0.75 \cdot 62, or 46.5, feet.
So when the length is {62\ {\rm ft}} and the width is {46.5\ {\rm ft}}, each pen has a maximum area of {2883\ {\rm ft^{2}}}.
The following graph illustrates our findings.
